stateEntry.name: default
stateEntry.description: The button's resting state when no interaction is occurring.

stateEntry.name: hover
stateEntry.description: Triggered when the user's pointer moves over the button. The background darkens by 8% to indicate interactivity. Not applicable on touch devices.

presentation.alt: A primary button with a slightly darker blue background indicating the hover state.

stateEntry.name: active
stateEntry.description: Triggered while the button is being pressed (mousedown or touch start). The background darkens by 16% from the default to indicate activation.

stateEntry.name: focus
stateEntry.description: Triggered when the button receives keyboard focus. A 2px focus ring appears with a 2px offset from the container edge. The focus ring uses the system focus color.

presentation.alt: A primary button with a visible 2px blue focus ring offset from the button edge by 2px.

stateEntry.name: disabled
stateEntry.description: The button is non-interactive. Opacity is reduced to 0.4. Pointer events are disabled. The button remains in the tab order when using aria-disabled instead of the HTML disabled attribute.

whenToUse.description: When the user cannot interact with the button because a precondition has not been met (e.g., required fields are empty).

whenNotToUse.description: When the button is processing an asynchronous operation.

alternative.name: loading
alternative.rationale: The loading state provides feedback that an action is in progress. A disabled button gives no feedback.

stateEntry.name: loading
stateEntry.description: The button label is replaced by a spinner animation. The button is non-interactive. The button maintains its dimensions from the default state to prevent layout shift.

presentation.alt: A user clicks a primary button labeled Save. The label is replaced by a spinner animation while the button maintains its width. After two seconds, the spinner is replaced by a checkmark and the label changes to Saved.
